channel.title: AIBC Blog
channel.description: Founder notes on AI coding, developer income, and monetizing your IDE.

item.title: My first $100 from coding ads — what the dashboard actually showed
item.description: Realistic timeline: impressions, hold period, payable balance, and the first manual payout request.

item.title: SEO for developers who hate marketing (write once, rank forever)
item.description: You don't need a growth team. You need honest posts targeting searches you already understand.

item.title: Claude Code uninstall anxiety — why clean restore matters
item.description: Patches that don't revert kill trust. How we restore index.js, settings.json, and ~/.aibc artifacts.

item.title: The Tuesday afternoon problem: building is easy, getting paid is hard
item.description: Parag Agrawal nailed it. The code works. Silence is worse than complaints. Here's what we optimize for.

item.title: What advertisers pay for inside your IDE (without popups)
item.description: Auction mechanics, CPM-style bids, and why dev-tool brands want spinner inventory.

item.title: Vibe coding is 10% code, 90% distribution (and how to fix that)
item.description: You can ship in a weekend. Getting found is the hard part. SEO, community, and monetizing while you build.

item.title: Refer a developer, earn $10: the math behind our referral program
item.description: Qualified when they earn $10 lifetime. Bonus hits your first payout. Simple rules, no MLM nonsense.

item.title: Founding member +5% forever — why we're capping at 15,000
item.description: Scarcity with a reason. First 15k authenticated devs get a permanent earnings multiplier.

item.title: Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code: where developer ads actually work
item.description: Not every surface is equal. Why the AI wait state beats banners, sidebars, and notification spam.

item.title: The $10 payout threshold — why we set it that way
item.description: Not arbitrary. Fraud review, Wise fees, and keeping the queue manageable for a small team.

item.title: Kickbacks vs AIBC: what actually pays developers in 2026
item.description: Same category, different choices. A straight comparison of developer share, caps, and restore behavior.

item.title: Make money while Claude thinks: an honest guide for junior devs
item.description: No get-rich-quick. Just how developer spinner monetization actually works for people starting out.

item.title: I coded 80% of my week with Claude — here's what I do with the other 20%
item.description: Build speed isn't the hard part anymore. Distribution, judgment, and getting paid while you ship — that's the other 20%.

item.title: Why your AI spinner is the most undervalued ad slot on the internet
item.description: Developers hate ads. They don't hate relevant one-liners while Claude thinks. Here's the economics.

item.title: Claude Code is creating a new generation of paid solopreneurs
item.description: Junior devs ship 10x faster with Claude. Here's why the spinner — not the side project — is where the money hides.
